ocr.text: Qualitative variables  Seizure-free (n=61)  Unfavorable (n=29)  P-value
Time from seizure onset to surgery ≤1 year  37  17  0.854
Seizure frequency (rare)  37  8  0.034
Site of surgery (left)  34  17  0.796
Gender (male)  31  16  0.699
Chemoradiotherapy  55  24  0.319
Variables  b  se  Wald test  df  OR  95 % CI for OR  p value
Duration of mechanical ventilation  0.052  0.032  2.615  1  1.054  0.989–1.123  0.106
Number of packed red blood cells transfused  0.531  0.287  3.413  1  1.701  0.968–2.988  0.065
In Table 1, the qualitative variables show that among 61 seizure-free patients, 37 experienced seizure onset to surgery within ≤1
year, while 17 of the 29 unfavorable patients had the same timing (P-value 0.854). In terms of seizure frequency, 37 seizure-free
patients had rare occurrences, compared to 8 unfavorable patients, with a significant P-value of 0.034. Regarding the site of
surgery, 34 seizure-free patients underwent left-side surgery, while 17 unfavorable patients did, resulting in a P-value of 0.796.
The gender distribution revealed that 31 seizure-free patients were male versus 16 unfavorable males, with a P-value of 0.699.
Lastly, 55 seizure-free patients received chemoradiotherapy compared to 24 unfavorable patients, yielding a P-value of 0.319.
Table 2 presents various variables with their statistical outcomes; for hemorrhagic shock, the coefficient b is 4.206, standard
error se is 1.646, Wald test value is 6.527, degrees of freedom df is 1, odds ratio OR is 67.1, confidence interval for OR is
2.663–1691, and p value is 0.011. For SAPS II score, b is 0.095, se is 0.042, Wald test is 5.092, df is 1, OR is 1.1, 95% CI is
Duration of mechanical ventilation shows b 0.052, se 0.032, Wald 2.615, df 1, OR 1.054, CI 0.989–1.123, p 0.106. The number
of packed red blood cells transfused has b 0.531, se 0.287, Wald 3.413, df 1, OR 1.701, CI 0.968–2.988, p 0.065. Lastly,
conservative treatment reveals b 0.207, se 1.101, Wald 0.035, df 1, OR 1.23, CI 0.142–10.65, and p 0.851.
